revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki)]][[en:Eda Shrine]][[fr:Eda-jinja]][[ja:江田神社 (宮崎市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Eda Shrine | native_name = 江田神社 | image = Eda-Jinjya Shrine Miyazaki.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Eda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|31.960442|131.464858}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|lt=Izanagi|ar|إزاناغي|az|İzanaqi|bcl|Izanagi|bn|ইজানাগি|bs|Izanagi|ca|Izanagi|cs|Izanagi a Izanami|da|Izanagi|el|Ιζανάγκι|en|Izanagi|eo|Izanagi|es|Izanagi|fa|ایزاناگی|fi|Izanaki|fr|Izanagi|he|איזאנגי|hr|Izanagi|hu|Izanagi|id|Izanagi|it|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|ka|იძანაგი|ko|이자나기|lt|Idzanagis|mk|Изанаги|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯒꯤ|no|Izanagi|or|ଇଜାନାଗି|pt|Izanagi|ro|Izanagi|ru|Идзанаги|simple|Izanagi|sk|Izanagi|sl|Izanagi|sr|Изанаки|sv|Izanagi|ta|இசநாகி|th|อิซานางิ|tl|Izanagi|tr|İzanagi|uk|Ідзанаґі|vi|Izanagi|zh|伊邪那岐|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Eda Shrine'''|江田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hyūga Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * Shrine|lt=Kirishimano Shrine|qid=Q135041487|1=Kirishimano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q204308|ltq=Q204308|qid=Q204308|1=Miyazaki (city)}} * Miyazaki|ja|宮崎郡|ko|미야자키군|zh|宮崎郡|qid=Q1132017|1=Miyazaki District, Miyazaki}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|31.960442|131.464858}} == image (P18) == * Eda-Jinjya Shrine Miyazaki.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Izanagi|lt=Izanagi|ar|إزاناغي|az|İzanaqi|bcl|Izanagi|bn|ইজানাগি|bs|Izanagi|ca|Izanagi|cs|Izanagi a Izanami|da|Izanagi|el|Ιζανάγκι|en|Izanagi|eo|Izanagi|es|Izanagi|fa|ایزاناگی|fi|Izanaki|fr|Izanagi|he|איזאנגי|hr|Izanagi|hu|Izanagi|id|Izanagi|it|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|ka|იძანაგი|ko|이자나기|lt|Idzanagis|mk|Изанаги|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯒꯤ|no|Izanagi|or|ଇଜାନାଗି|pt|Izanagi|ro|Izanagi|ru|Идзанаги|simple|Izanagi|sk|Izanagi|sl|Izanagi|sr|Изанаки|sv|Izanagi|ta|இசநாகி|th|อิซานางิ|tl|Izanagi|tr|İzanagi|uk|Ідзанаґі|vi|Izanagi|zh|伊邪那岐|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}} https://web.archive.org/web/20160306093354/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/640202.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183326 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|lt=nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|fr|Nagare-zukuri|it|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|pl|Nagare-zukuri|ru|Нагарэ-дзукури|sv|Nagare-zukuri|zh|流造|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9350005000761 == Commons category (P373) == * Eda-jinja (Miyazaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮崎県宮崎市阿波岐原町字産母127', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/0kg2pw2https://developers.google.com/freebase/ {{commons category|Category:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki)}} [[EN:Eda Shrine]] [[FR:Eda-jinja]] [[JA:江田神社 (宮崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q3047222}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Eda Shrine |Image = [[File:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300|qid=Q130300|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Miyazaki City|Q204308|qid=Q204308|1=Miyazaki (city)}} Awagihara-chō, Aza Yabo 127 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} |Shintai = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=November 22|Q3025|qid=Q3025|1=November 22}} |Rituals = Chinowa kuguri ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}}) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyazaki|label=Eda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Eda Shrine''' (えだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Awagihara-chō|Q132132174|qid=Q132132174|1=Awagihara-chō}} (Awagihara-chō), {{ill|lt=Miyazaki City|Q204308|qid=Q204308|1=Miyazaki (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300|qid=Q130300|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === '''Main enshrined deity''' * '''{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}''' (いざなぎのみこと) '''Associated enshrined deity''' * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} (いざなみのみこと) – Enshrined additionally in {{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|qid=Q598057|1=Juei}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1183|Q19723|qid=Q19723|1=1183}}). These two deities are collectively called “Yabo Nihashira Daimyōjin” (産母二柱大明神), and are also affectionately referred to by local people as “Yabo-sama.” === History === === Mythology === According to the '{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}' and '{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}', Izanagi-no-Mikoto returned from {{ill|lt=Yomi|Q1142558|qid=Q1142558|1=Yomi}} and performed a {{ill|lt=misogi|Q1936265|qid=Q1936265|1=Misogi}} purification ritual. Regarding the location where the misogi was performed, the 'Kojiki' records it as “Awagihara of the small inlet of Tachibana in Hyūga of Tsukushi,” while the 'Nihon Shoki' records it as “Awagihara (Aki-bara) of the small inlet of Tachibana in Hyūga of Tsukushi.” This Awagihara / Aki-bara is traditionally identified as the present site. This place name is also recited at the beginning of the {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|qid=Q1760119|1=Norito}} chanted at shrines throughout Japan: “Kakemaku mo kashikoki Izanagi no Ōkami, Tsukushi no Hyūga no Tachibana no Odo no Awagihara ni misogi-harae tamai shi toki ni…”. For this reason, the area is regarded as “the birthplace of purification rituals.”{{Cite web|ja|url= http://www.kanko-miyazaki.jp/shinwanofurusato/sp/img/pamph/pdf/01.pdf |title= Hyūga Mythology Journey |website= Miyazaki Prefecture |accessdate= 2021-11-07 }} The nearby “Misogi Pond” is said to be the place where Izanagi-no-Mikoto performed the purification. It was once an inlet, but later reclaimed and named “Eda.” Based on the above mythology, the area is also believed to be the birthplace of Izanagi’s children {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|qid=Q595520|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}—collectively known as the {{ill|lt=Three Precious Children|Q4702904|qid=Q4702904|1=Three Noble Children}}—as well as the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}. Numerous ancient settlement remains are found around the shrine, including the representative Aoki Site dating to the early {{ill|lt=Yayoi period|Q207858|qid=Q207858|1=Yayoi period}} (about 2,400 years ago). From Aoki No. 1 Tumulus, an early {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q11268718|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} keyhole-shaped burial mound dating to the late 3rd century, the largest wooden burial chamber and coffin remains in Japan have been discovered. === Historical overview === The earliest mention in national histories appears in the '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}', under the entry for the 1st day of the 8th month of {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=837|Q25093|qid=Q25093|1=837}}): “Tsuno Shrine of Koyu District, Hyūga Province; Tsuma Shrine; '''Eda Shrine''' of Miyazaki District; Kirishima Mine Shrine of Morokata District—all placed among the official shrines.” In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Eda Shrine” in Miyazaki District of Hyūga Province, and classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. The '{{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}' records an “Eda Village” in Miyazaki District, and Sue ware from around the 10th century has been excavated from the precincts, suggesting that the shrine still stands on its original site since its founding. By {{ill|lt=Tenroku|Q1327229|qid=Q1327229|1=Tenroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=970|Q24047|qid=Q24047|1=970}}), its {{ill|lt=court rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} had risen to the highest level of {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}, and it is said to have once flourished with a magnificent shrine building bearing the chrysanthemum crest. In the medieval period it belonged to {{ill|lt=Naka District (Miyazaki)|Q10978107|qid=Q10978107|1=Naka district}}, and is mentioned in Kyoto’s “Kiyotaki Shrine Kanjin Jinmyōchō” as well as the “Kaidanin Official Shrine Register” of {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1479|Q6620|qid=Q6620|1=1479}}), indicating that it was regarded as one of the representative shrines of Hyūga Province. During the Edo period, it was under shogunal land, the {{ill|lt=Itō clan|Q2975280|qid=Q2975280|1=Itō clan}}, and the {{ill|lt=Obi Domain|Q8192240|qid=Q8192240|1=Obi Domain}} as entrusted territory. However, after losing its buildings to the great tsunami caused by the {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1662|Q7574|qid=Q7574|1=1662}}) {{ill|lt=Tondokoro Earthquake|Q11430459|qid=Q11430459|1=1662 Hyūga-nada earthquake}}, the shrine declined and came to be treated as the tutelary shrine of a single village. It is also said that many valuable historical artifacts were lost to the tsunami. In the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the Shinto scholar {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|qid=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} wrote in his 'Shokoku Ichinomiya Junpaiki': “I visited the shrine of Eda, then worshiped at Sumiyoshi of Awagihara. When I inquired, I was told that the pine trees of Sumiyoshi are the pine groves of Awagihara. On this seashore, Izanagi-no-Mikoto performed his purification.” On May 25, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine, and on February 9 of the 40th year, it was designated a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. === Court ranks === * {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=837|Q25093|qid=Q25093|1=837}}) 8th month – Ranked among official shrines ({{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}) * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 2 ({{ill|lt=858|Q30371|qid=Q30371|1=858}}) October 22 – Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Junior Fourth Rank Lower ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) === Precincts === === Main shrine === File:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki) honden.JPG|Main hall File:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki) sandou.JPG|Approach === Sacred pond === [[File:Eda-jinja (Miyazaki) Miike-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Sacred Pond (Misogi Pond)]] The pond where Izanagi-no-Mikoto is said to have performed the misogi is located northeast of the shrine ({{Coord|31|57|50.19|N|131|28|02.92|E|region:JP-45_type:landmark|name=Sacred Pond (Misogi Pond)}}), and is called “Miike” or “Misogi Pond.”The name of the pond is based on the on-site explanatory sign. File:Misogi goten.JPG|Misogi Hall === Festivals === * Kinen-sai (February 17)Festival information based on on-site explanatory sign. * Ōharae-sai (June 30) *: As a special ritual, Chinowa kuguri is performed.On-site explanatory sign. In the past, it was customary to perform a beach purification at the shore on the previous night.“Place Names of Miyazaki Prefecture,” entry on Eda Shrine. On the day of the ritual, after the mikoshi procession, priests, parishioners, and worshippers pass through the chinowa in that order. * Grand Annual Festival (November 22) * Niiname-sai (November 23) At the Spring Shaden Festival and the Autumn Grand Festival, “Eda Kagura” is dedicated. The history of this kagura is ancient, and kagura masks inscribed with the year Keichō 17 still remain. === Local information === '''Address''' * Awagihara-chō, Aza Yabo 127, {{ill|lt=Miyazaki City|Q204308|qid=Q204308|1=Miyazaki (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|Q130300|qid=Q130300|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Miyazaki Kōtsū|Q11454180|qid=Q11454180|1=Miyazaki Kotsu}} bus (Route 19, Zoo Line), get off at “Eda Shrine” bus stop (a short walk after alighting) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Miyazaki City)|Q11381866|qid=Q11381866|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} – Enshrines the Sumiyoshi Sanjin, said to have been born during Izanagi-no-Mikoto’s purification. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * On-site explanatory signs * “Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Miyazaki Prefecture” ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Miyazaki City, entry on Eda Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Birth of the gods#Misogi purification and the birth of the Three Precious Children|Q3545159|qid=Q3545159|1=Kamiumi}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Eda-jinja_(Miyazaki)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/640202.html Eda Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Compilation”) * [https://eda-jinnja7.webnode.jp/ Official Eda Shrine website] {{Shinto navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Eda Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Eda Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hyūga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:宮崎市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hyūga Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazaki (city)]]
